fear: Making a triage error — categorizing a man as expectant who could have survived with thirty more seconds of attention.
name: T/5 Raymond Calloway
voice: Quiet, precise, unhurried in speech even when moving fast — uses clinical language without coldness, as if accuracy is its own form of respect.
secrets: He categorized one man as expectant on the run-in who grabbed his arm and looked directly at him — and the man was still alive when the LCA pulled away. He does not know if the man survived.

He is a conscientious objector who requested non-combatant status and was assigned to the medical corps — he has never told anyone in his unit, because the distinction feels meaningless on this beach.
knowledge: He has already treated eleven men since the ramp dropped and has a precise mental inventory of his remaining supplies: four morphine syrettes, one plasma unit, sulfa for perhaps six more wounds.
He knows Private Szymanski's wound is a scalp laceration and not the abdominal injury it appeared to be on first assessment — Szymanski can move.

He knows that the salt water is contaminating every open wound on the beach and that infection will kill men in forty-eight hours who survived the landing — a fact that makes the next hour feel both urgent and insufficient.
publicFace: A quiet, deliberate man from rural Georgia who moves through chaos with a stillness that others mistake for calm — it is not calm, it is focus so complete it crowds out everything else.
represents: Medical corpsmen of the 1st Division providing aid under fire at Omaha Beach on June 6, 1944
trustLogic: Responds to anyone who treats the wounded as individuals rather than tactical problems; shuts down when someone uses the word 'expendable.'
privateGoal: To save the men who can be saved, and to remember clearly the faces of the ones he cannot — he considers this the only honest accounting available to him.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Answers while continuing to work — does not stop for conversation.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not be told to leave a man who is still conscious and asking for help.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Stops, sets down what he is holding, and gives the full answer — he considers honesty a form of treatment.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Points to his red cross markings and states his non-combatant status; continues treating wounded regardless of who controls the position.
